name: io.github.06ketan/medium-ops
description: Medium CLI + 23-tool MCP server. Your IDE drafts replies. No API keys.
title: medium-ops

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_INTEGRATION_TOKEN
environmentVariables.description: Legacy Medium Integration Token (api.medium.com/v1/*). Optional. Generate at medium.com/me/settings → 'Integration tokens' (note: Medium stopped issuing new tokens in 2023).

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_SID
environmentVariables.description: Medium 'sid' cookie value from medium.com (Application → Cookies). Used for authenticated reads + dashboard GraphQL.

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_UID
environmentVariables.description: Medium 'uid' cookie value. Required alongside sid for some authenticated endpoints.

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_XSRF
environmentVariables.description: Medium 'xsrf' cookie value. Required for any dashboard write (post_response, publish_post, delete_post, draft updates).

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_CF_CLEARANCE
environmentVariables.description: Cloudflare 'cf_clearance' cookie. Only required when Cloudflare challenges your IP for dashboard write calls.

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_USERNAME
environmentVariables.description: Your Medium handle (without the @). Required for the public RSS read path so the client knows whose feed to fetch.

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_OPS_MCP_PATH
environmentVariables.description: Override path to the mcp.json file the auth layer reads. Defaults to ~/.cursor/mcp.json.

environmentVariables.name: MEDIUM_OPS_LLM_CMD
environmentVariables.description: Optional: override the host CLI used by the unattended daemon path (default: auto-detect claude / cursor-agent / codex on PATH). Use {prompt} placeholder if your CLI takes the prompt as an arg.
